Deliver formatter fixes as GitHub fenced `suggestion` blocks in inline review comments on the existing PR, not as a separate formatting PR or bot-pushed branch.

Compute formatter output independently from the PR workspace via a configured formatter command and deterministic unified-diff mapping rather than consuming Jenkins artifacts or asking the agent to invent hunks.
Keep explicit formatter-suggestion requests available while `review.formatterSuggestions.automatic` defaults off and remains deferred until a later opt-in milestone wires automatic review inclusion.
Publish accepted formatter suggestions as one batched same-PR Pull Request Review with multiple inline suggestion comments and idempotency markers.
Treat unsafe, unmappable, malformed, off-diff, and excessive formatter hunks as visible structured skips/caps instead of guessing target ranges.

A non-pass validation artifact can still contain useful authoritative evidence, but closeout must independently resolve whether flagged items are completion blockers; here the Boundary Map defect is an artifact-quality deviation and R086 is intentionally deferred rather than required for explicit-mode success.
Exact test paths matter for Bun: `src/execution/verify-m066-s05.test.ts` was stale in prior notes, while the actual verifier test lives at `scripts/verify-m066-s05.test.ts`.
nodes.description: Source: M053 closeout verification/S05 live-proof docs and verifier checks

nodes.label: Automatic formatter suggestions need a separate runtime proof milestone; default-off config and documentation are insufficient to claim automatic normal-review inclusion.

Roadmap Boundary Map drift can be recovered for validation from slice SUMMARY `provides`/`requires`, but an empty duplicated roadmap section weakens closeout evidence and should be fixed in planning/rendering before future milestones rely on it.

For exact same-PR suggestion proof, combine deterministic local contract tests with a bounded live proof record that includes trigger action, delivery/reviewOutputKey correlation, review state, and fenced suggestion comments.
Keep formatter suggestion generation as three composable seams: command execution, conservative unified-diff-to-PR-line mapping, and same-PR review publication.

In combined review-and-format orchestration, preserve independent subflow outcomes: formatter diagnostics stay visible without suppressing review behavior, and review failures should not prevent an available formatter subflow from running.
Caps should be applied after safety validation so skip/cap counts remain truthful and downstream diagnostics can distinguish unsafe hunks from accepted-but-excess suggestions.

The milestone ID and project history contain stale cross-references to M066 for the same formatter-suggestion capability, so closeout had to reconcile M053 artifacts with existing M066 requirement and project-state evidence.
The roadmap has two empty `## Boundary Map` headings even though the slice summaries carry enough producer/consumer contracts to prove integration.

A non-pass validation artifact can still contain useful authoritative evidence, but closeout must independently resolve whether flagged items are completion blockers; here the Boundary Map defect is an artifact-quality deviation and R086 is intentionally deferred rather than required for explicit-mode success.
Exact test paths matter for Bun: `src/execution/verify-m066-s05.test.ts` was stale in prior notes, while the actual verifier test lives at `scripts/verify-m066-s05.test.ts`.

Deliver formatter fixes as GitHub suggestion blocks in inline Pull Request Review comments on the existing PR, not as a separate PR, branch push, bot-authored commit, issue comment, or standalone comment loop.
Generate formatter suggestion content with a deterministic repository-configured formatter command and unified-diff mapping pipeline instead of relying on Jenkins artifacts or Claude-authored hunks.
Keep explicit formatter-suggestion requests always available while `review.formatterSuggestions.automatic` defaults false and remains reserved for future automatic-review inclusion.
Publish formatter suggestions as one batched same-PR Pull Request Review with multiple inline comments, idempotency markers, and all-or-nothing publisher outcomes.
Use one shared `mention-format-suggestions` action identity from mention routing through formatter subflow key generation, completion logs, smoke artifact, and verifier proof.

Live proof can fail before GitHub suggestion acceptance: S06 showed the deployed app acknowledged `@kodiai format suggestions` but handled it as generic conversation without formatter subflow fields or review-output identity.
PR-head formatter configuration is required for controlled smokes when `main` does not contain `review.formatterSuggestions.command`, because Kodiai loads config from the checked-out PR head.
The inbound sanitize pipeline strips HTML comments, so formatter publication must preserve idempotency markers with raw secret scanning plus targeted outgoing mention sanitization instead.
Verification-first smoke artifacts prevent false greens: S05 and S06 recorded blocked/negative proof rather than fabricating `m066_s05_ok`, and R077/R085 only moved after S07's live verifier passed.

The local GSD memory store was malformed/unwritable during the milestone, so reusable decisions and gotchas had to be preserved in slice summaries, PROJECT.md, and this LEARNINGS artifact when `capture_thought` failed.

Use a verifier-first operational proof gate for external platform claims: `verify:m066:s05` accepts only a formatter review-output key, exactly one COMMENTED same-PR Pull Request Review, and an associated fenced suggestion review comment.

Split formatter suggestions into composable seams: mention-intent parsing, command execution, diff parsing, PR diff commentability indexing, suggestion mapping, orchestration, and publisher each have bounded contracts and targeted tests.
Treat invalid formatter diff states as structured skip diagnostics instead of partial guesses, and enforce caps after safety validation so generated/skipped/capped counts stay truthful.
Keep format-only formatter suggestions read-only and Claude-free while combined review-and-format requests run normal review and formatter subflows independently with separate failure surfaces.

Bounded deployed-smoke diagnostics should report trigger classification, formatter command, mapper, publisher, GitHub acceptance, delivery id, and reviewOutputKey separately so the next remediation starts at the first failing boundary.

The initial authenticated live trigger reached the deployed app and received a bot acknowledgement, but the deployed runtime still handled it as a generic conversational formatting request rather than explicit formatter intent.

Current source already handled the plain trigger; the actionable regression target was missing formatter completion evidence (`deliveryId`, `reviewOutputKey`, `reviewOutputAction`) rather than pure phrase classification.
The Azure Container Apps deploy path hit a transient Azure CLI connection reset during secret-reference update, but the documented idempotent retry completed and produced the active proof revision.

Live proof can fail before GitHub suggestion acceptance: S06 showed the deployed app acknowledged `@kodiai format suggestions` but handled it as generic conversation without formatter subflow fields or review-output identity.
PR-head formatter configuration is required for controlled smokes when `main` does not contain `review.formatterSuggestions.command`, because Kodiai loads config from the checked-out PR head.
The inbound sanitize pipeline strips HTML comments, so formatter publication must preserve idempotency markers with raw secret scanning plus targeted outgoing mention sanitization instead.
Verification-first smoke artifacts prevent false greens: S05 and S06 recorded blocked/negative proof rather than fabricating `m066_s05_ok`, and R077/R085 only moved after S07's live verifier passed.

The local GSD memory store was malformed/unwritable during the milestone, so reusable decisions and gotchas had to be preserved in slice summaries, PROJECT.md, and this LEARNINGS artifact when `capture_thought` failed.

Keep review orchestration contracts in `src/review-orchestration/` while `src/handlers/review.ts` remains the production coordinator and owner of side effects. This preserves the current production spine while making plan, reducer, and candidate contracts testable.
Start `ReviewPlan` as a typed projection of existing review decisions rather than a new routing owner. This avoided hidden behavior changes while creating a future seam for routing/policy ownership.

Add candidate finding as a shadow-only MCP/tool/artifact seam in M067 while leaving inline GitHub publication as the production-visible path. This lets later milestones prove candidate schemas before changing publication behavior.

Extract current post-processing gates into `ReviewReducerResult` first and keep reducer uncertainty fail-open with no destructive inline cleanup. This made reducer behavior observable without combining refactor risk with publication-policy changes.

Use `xbmc/xbmc#28172` as the production-shaped proof target, but treat its missing Review Details publication as blocked evidence rather than green acceptance. The target choice remains documented but should be revisited for future live proof because repo trigger config disabled synchronize publication.

Restrict S05 acceptance proof to automatic review-handler keys and reject mention-review keys by default. This remains the right proof lane because M067 wiring lives in the automatic review handler path.

Live proof target configuration can invalidate an otherwise valid production-shaped key. The captured `xbmc/xbmc#28172` synchronize key was structurally valid, but checked-out repo config did not enable synchronize-triggered reviews, so no Review Details artifact was published.
A blocked live acceptance requirement must not be collapsed into a validated safety constraint. R099 remains blocked while R100 is validated by zero visible artifacts and no additional GitHub write.

Verifier failures can be useful acceptance evidence only when classified as a documented fail-closed blocker, not as green proof. `M067-S05-PUBLICATION-READINESS` / `review_details_not_published` now means stop before retrying visible GitHub output.

Credential-free verifiers kept S01-S04 contract proof repeatable even when live GitHub/Azure proof was blocked. Fresh closeout reran S01-S04 JSON verifiers and the S05 unit suite without needing to publish anything.

The local GSD memory store was unhealthy during multiple slices, so reusable knowledge had to be preserved in slice summaries and milestone learnings instead of durable memory rows. Future sessions should repair the malformed memory database if memory persistence is needed.

Introduce orchestration changes as bounded projections before moving production authority. ReviewPlan, graph-validation state, reducer results, and candidate metadata all expose typed observability while preserving current routing/publication behavior.

Public Review Details should use one compact line per subsystem and keep deeper state in logs, snapshots, verifier output, or private artifacts. M067 applied this to plan, reducer, and candidate surfaces.

Fail open for diagnostic/shadow seams, but fail closed for safety-sensitive publication retries. Builder/reducer/candidate failures preserve review execution, while missing exact-key Review Details publication blocks further GitHub writes.

Use exact-key publication-readiness preflight before repeating live proof attempts. The preflight checks normalized repo/PR/action/head identity and visible artifact state before any new trigger is considered.

Keep shadow MCP collector state executor-owned when stateless per-request server factories need per-review accumulation. S04 used executor-owned candidate collector state and attached bounded metadata to every executor return branch.
The final proof target skipped review publication even after deployment and valid automatic key capture because target repo configuration did not enable synchronize reviews.
The milestone could validate no visible-output expansion while failing the live Review Details publication criterion. Zero exact-key artifacts proved safety, not observability.
`slog` and the github-bot secret helper were unavailable during S05 execution, so GitHub write evidence had to be recorded in the bounded smoke artifact using the existing authenticated `gh` path.
Multiple attempts to persist reusable memories failed with malformed/failed GSD memory-store errors, making `M067-LEARNINGS.md` the practical audit trail for this closeout.

Chose to establish the first specialist lane as a pure local `docs-config-truth` contract before wiring runtime handler behavior, because the active checkout lacked the previously referenced review-orchestration/candidate modules and needed a trustworthy local seam first.
Chose same-job, read-only, injectable shadow specialist execution in `createReviewHandler` rather than a separate ACA specialist job or visible GitHub publication path.

Chose a private aggregate metric reducer for specialist candidate evidence, exposing bounded counts/availability/correlation/denial fields while excluding candidate bodies, prompts, model output, tool payloads, approval fields, and publication fields.
Chose aggregate-only compact Review Details/log/verifier projection, with line-only caps and critical redaction/publication-denial fields ordered before long correlation values.

Chose a separate live-required `verify:m069:s05` proof gate where `--allow-blocked` changes only process exit behavior; blocked evidence remains `success:false` and cannot count as operational success.

Local fixture/static proof is necessary but not sufficient for operational acceptance; S04 can return `m069_ok` for bounded contract evidence while S05 remains blocked on exact live GitHub/Log Analytics correlation.
Fail-open specialist integration needs tests for success, skipped, timeout, thrown error, and malformed/unclassifiable outputs so normal review completion stays authoritative.

Publication-boundary proof must include negative tests with malicious specialist-shaped output, not only absence of required publication wiring, because safety depends on forbidding raw candidate leakage and visible specialist comments/approvals.

Compact evidence can accidentally hide safety fields when long correlation values consume the line budget; critical redaction and publication-denial fields must be ordered before long identifiers and protected by regression tests.

Live-access blockers must be reported as blockers with explicit reason strings such as `github_access_404;missing_correlation_key`, not silently downgraded into fixture success or milestone operational success.
Use pure typed contract modules plus synthetic JSON verifiers for the first slice of a risky integration milestone, then consume that contract from handler, reducer, evidence, and live-proof slices.
Keep shadow subflows injectable and read-only, and normalize every timeout/error/malformed result into bounded private status before the main review executor proceeds.
Project untrusted specialist output into aggregate metrics at a single reducer boundary, then feed only that projection to logs, Review Details, and verifiers.
Separate local verifier status vocabulary from live-required proof vocabulary so fixture success cannot be mislabeled as production proof.
For production-like proof gates, provide deterministic injected collectors for tests while the default CLI path collects real GitHub/runtime evidence and classifies access/config gaps explicitly.
The active checkout lacked some earlier milestone module names, so the safest path was to reconcile with a new local `src/specialists/` contract before integration.
Planned `src/review-audit/*` paths were absent during S05 closeout, but the authoritative command still exited 0 using the available focused S05 verifier tests and guard suites.

The validation artifact marked operational acceptance as needs-attention because the live-required proof returned blocked evidence, even though the roadmap criterion explicitly permits recording credential/config/live-access blockers as blocked evidence rather than success.
